book_claims.claim: Vegetable used in the Longevity Diet fennel salad and as a side dish ingredient.
book_claims.mechanism: Provides dietary fiber, vitamin C, and potassium as part of a plant-forward longevity eating pattern.

supplementary_research.finding: Anethole, the principal volatile compound in fennel, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may contribute to reduced chronic disease risk. (PMID 22849965)
Fennel seed extract demonstrated antioxidant activity and potential chemopreventive effects in in vitro models, supporting inclusion of fennel in longevity-oriented diets. (PMID 31960481)
Fennel contains phytoestrogens, antioxidant polyphenols, and dietary fiber; regular consumption is associated with digestive health and reduced inflammation.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Water-soluble; best consumed raw or lightly cooked
Mix of soluble and insoluble fiber; supports gut microbiome
Higher in fennel seeds; anti-inflammatory and antioxidant

synergies.description: Fat-soluble antioxidants in fennel are better absorbed when combined with olive oil
Classic pairing in Mediterranean salads; vitamin C from lemon enhances iron absorption from fennel
Traditional Italian pairing; fennel's anise notes complement oily fish and provide complementary phytonutrients
